findings.summary: 2 embedded base64 rasters inside the SVG (image/jpeg, image/png). The SVG mime type promises infinite scale, but the actual pixels are locked to one resolution — usually larger than a plain PNG.
evidence: SVG byte size: 592 bytes (includes the base64 overhead)
re-author the raster region as vector paths, or split it out to its own asset

suggested_actions.description: Re-export the SVG from the design tool with the raster layer flattened to vector geometry, or split the embedded image out to its own PNG / WebP asset referenced by URL.

findings.summary: generateInvoice is 204 lines long (domain function threshold 60). Bodies this size usually mix unrelated responsibilities, and an agent editing one section often misses interactions in another.

suggested_actions.description: Extract cohesive sections into named helpers so each responsibility can be read, tested, and edited in isolation.

findings.summary: 8 direct uses of Date.now()/new Date(). Reading the system clock in domain code makes behaviour non-deterministic and couples tests to wall time.
evidence: each call observes wall time at runtime — tests cannot pin a fixed moment without monkey-patching

suggested_actions.description: Inject a clock/now() abstraction so domain code is deterministic in tests and free of hidden temporal coupling.

findings.summary: 1 receiver using both UTC and local Date methods. The two families differ silently by the host's UTC offset.
evidence: pick one family per variable; usually UTC for storage and local only at the display boundary

suggested_actions.description: Convert all reads on this receiver to either the UTC family or the local family — don't mix.

findings.summary: Repo ships no agent-readable instruction files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md, .agents/skills/*/SKILL.md). Agents loading this repo will not see project commands, conventions, or safety rules and are more likely to make confident-but-wrong edits.
evidence: no AGENTS.md found at repo root
no CLAUDE.md found at repo root
package.json declares bin(s): messy — agents have no way to discover commands

suggested_actions.description: Add AGENTS.md or a Claude skill so coding agents can discover repo conventions before editing.

findings.summary: 1 hardcoded dev-server URL (`localhost:NNNN` / `127.0.0.1:NNNN` / `0.0.0.0:NNNN`) in non-test, non-config source. These literals only work against whichever dev server happens to be running on that port — every other environment silently fails to connect.
evidence: move the value behind a config / env var (`process.env.API_URL`, settings module, etc.)

suggested_actions.description: Replace the literal with a config-supplied URL (`process.env.API_URL`, a settings module, or the framework's runtime config). Keep the dev-server URL in `.env.local` / `.env.example` so each environment supplies its own.

findings.summary: 1 hardcoded user-home path (`/Users/<name>/`, `/home/<name>/`, or `C:\Users\<name>\`) in non-test source. These paths exist on exactly one machine — the code silently breaks for every other developer, every CI runner, and every deploy.
evidence: replace with `os.homedir()`, `process.env.HOME`, or a config-driven base path

suggested_actions.description: Replace the literal with `os.homedir()` (or `process.env.HOME`) and join the remainder, or pass the base path through configuration so each environment supplies its own.

findings.summary: 7 direct uses of Date.now()/new Date(). Reading the system clock in domain code makes behaviour non-deterministic and couples tests to wall time.
evidence: each call observes wall time at runtime — tests cannot pin a fixed moment without monkey-patching

suggested_actions.description: Inject a clock/now() abstraction so domain code is deterministic in tests and free of hidden temporal coupling.

findings.summary: 2 `new Date("…")` calls parsing a string with no timezone marker. The runtime applies its own timezone, which is rarely the one the literal author had in mind.
evidence: add `Z` for UTC, `±HH:MM` for an offset, or parse through a timezone-aware library

suggested_actions.description: Append `Z` (UTC) or `±HH:MM` to the literal, or parse through a library that mandates an explicit zone.

findings.summary: Test "renders the enterprise plan" contains no expect/assert calls. A test that only runs setup gives agents false confidence.

suggested_actions.description: Assert the observable behaviour this test is meant to protect, or delete the test if it only exercises setup.

findings.summary: Domain literal "enterprise" appears in 4 production files across 4 areas. Repeated literals often become duplicated policy.
evidence: appears in 4 production files across src, src/api, src/jobs, src/ui
representative files: src/api/plan.ts:2, src/billing.ts:7, src/jobs/plan-sync.ts:2, src/ui/pricing.ts:2

suggested_actions.description: Move the literal to a named constant, enum, schema, registry, or policy module before adding another copy.

findings.summary: calculateRefundPreview has a safe-sounding name but appears to perform side effects. Misleading names make edits riskier because callers may treat the function as pure.
evidence: name prefix suggests a pure transformation
3 side-effect signals in the function body

suggested_actions.description: Rename the function to disclose the side effect, or extract the pure calculation/read from the mutation.

findings.summary: 1 day-level millisecond constant in arithmetic. A day isn't 86,400,000 ms on DST-transition days; manual ms math drifts silently.
evidence: use a calendar-aware library (Luxon, Temporal, date-fns-tz) for day math

suggested_actions.description: Replace ms-arithmetic with a timezone-aware addDays/subtract operation that knows about DST and leap years.

findings.summary: 4 aliases from the "tenant" concept group appear across 5 files. An agent extending one vocabulary may duplicate logic that already exists under another name.
evidence: aliases found: account, organisation, team, workspace

suggested_actions.description: Pick or document the canonical term, and use aliases deliberately rather than accidentally.

findings.summary: File is 310 lines (threshold 300). Modules this large hide local coupling: small edits can collide with code an agent never loaded into context.
evidence: 5 top-level functions declared in this file

suggested_actions.description: Split along clear responsibility boundaries. Smaller modules give humans and agents a smaller surface to reason about per edit.

findings.summary: Comment appears to carry a rule that nearby code does not obviously encode. Hidden prose rules are easy for agents and new maintainers to miss.
evidence: comment says: "Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves."
domain terms not found nearby: owner, plan, billing

suggested_actions.description: Move the rule into a named guard, type, config value, or test; keep comments for rationale.

findings.summary: Destination /settings/billing appears in 2 nav source files with 2 different labels. An agent updating one source will leave the others stale.

suggested_actions.description: Move destination metadata into one source of truth or document which nav file is canonical.

findings.summary: 14 TODO/FIXME markers (424.2 per 1k LOC). Each marker is intended behaviour the author flagged as not-final — too many in one file makes it hard for an agent to tell which logic is load-bearing.

suggested_actions.description: Convert each marker into a tracked issue or remove it. Lingering TODOs mislead humans and agents about which behaviour is intentional.

findings.summary: Route /settings/billing appears to be labelled in 4 competing ways across its path, file, component, page title, and nav entries. An agent editing one label may leave the others out of sync.

suggested_actions.description: Choose the canonical name for this destination and align route metadata, nav labels, and page/component names.

findings.summary: 253.9 KB raster asset (threshold 200 / 500 / 1000 KB). Heavy images directly inflate page weight and Core Web Vitals; the fix is usually a resize plus a modern-format re-encode, not a build change.
evidence: severity ladder: low ≥ 200 KB · medium ≥ 500 KB · high ≥ 1000 KB
consider resizing to the rendered dimensions and re-encoding to webp or avif

suggested_actions.description: Resize the image to the dimensions it actually renders at and re-encode as WebP or AVIF. If the asset is decorative, consider whether an SVG icon or CSS gradient would replace it entirely.

findings.summary: 1 local link in docs/teams.md point at file(s) that do not exist on disk. Agents reading this doc may follow stale instructions.

suggested_actions.description: Update the docs or restore the referenced file so agents do not follow stale instructions.

findings.summary: 3 synchronous I/O calls inside domain function — each invocation blocks the event loop for the duration of the I/O. Async counterparts exist for every method captured.
evidence: swap for the async variant (`readFile`, `writeFile`, `exec`, …) and `await` it

suggested_actions.description: Replace the `*Sync` call with its async counterpart and `await` it. The function may need to be marked `async`; React components should move the I/O into a Server Component / loader rather than the render body.

findings.summary: 1 direct use of Date.now()/new Date(). Reading the system clock in domain code makes behaviour non-deterministic and couples tests to wall time.
evidence: each call observes wall time at runtime — tests cannot pin a fixed moment without monkey-patching

suggested_actions.description: Inject a clock/now() abstraction so domain code is deterministic in tests and free of hidden temporal coupling.

findings.summary: 32×32 PNG icon-sized raster. SVG would scale cleanly across DPIs and ship smaller; the current asset locks the image to a single resolution.
evidence: consider replacing with an SVG icon — same render at every DPI, smaller bytes

suggested_actions.description: Re-author or re-export the icon as an SVG. If the source is a designer's PNG, the original vector likely exists in Figma / Sketch / Illustrator — re-export from there.

findings.summary: 1 hand-rolled string concatenation using Date methods. Manual formatting routinely drops zero-padding, timezone, or zero-indexed month handling.

suggested_actions.description: Replace the concatenation with `d.toISOString()`, `Intl.DateTimeFormat(locale, opts).format(d)`, or a timezone-aware library's formatter.

findings.summary: 1 `toLocale*()` call with no locale argument. Output varies by the runtime's default locale, not by anything the code controls.
evidence: pass an explicit locale (e.g. `'en-US'`) or use Intl.DateTimeFormat

suggested_actions.description: Pass an explicit BCP-47 locale string, or move to `Intl.DateTimeFormat` with locale + options.

findings.summary: 3 boolean-typed declarations without a recognised boolean prefix. Booleans read as nouns when unprefixed; `is`/`has`/`should`/`can` makes intent obvious to the reader.
evidence: built-in React-state names (loading/ready/active/…) are exempt; add project-specific names via `detectors.options.boolean_naming_drift.allowedNames`

suggested_actions.description: Rename to `is*` / `has*` / `should*` / `can*` (or add to `detectors.options.boolean_naming_drift.allowedNames` if the current name is a project idiom).

findings.summary: 2 boolean-typed declarations without a recognised boolean prefix. Booleans read as nouns when unprefixed; `is`/`has`/`should`/`can` makes intent obvious to the reader.
evidence: built-in React-state names (loading/ready/active/…) are exempt; add project-specific names via `detectors.options.boolean_naming_drift.allowedNames`

suggested_actions.description: Rename to `is*` / `has*` / `should*` / `can*` (or add to `detectors.options.boolean_naming_drift.allowedNames` if the current name is a project idiom).

findings.summary: Test "creates an invoice" only uses weak assertion matchers. It may prove the code ran, not that behaviour is protected.

suggested_actions.description: Assert the observable behaviour this test is meant to protect, or delete the test if it only exercises setup.

findings.summary: describeCheckout accepts a broad `options` object with an implicit shape. Generic bags make it hard for agents to know which fields are required.
evidence: 6 distinct property reads: currency, plan, region, retry, role, status

suggested_actions.description: Replace the generic bag with a named type/object shape, or destructure only the fields this function owns.

findings.summary: resolvePaymentResult returns several object shapes without an explicit return type. Callers and agents may infer the wrong result contract.
evidence: example keys: { code, message, ok } vs { permanent, url }

suggested_actions.description: Add an explicit return type or split branch-specific results into named result variants.

findings.summary: Conditional combines 2 negative flags. Double-negative logic is easy to invert during maintenance.

suggested_actions.description: Prefer positive flag names or extract the predicate into a named helper before extending this condition.

findings.summary: 2 declarations whose plural form disagrees with the annotated type's array shape.
evidence: v1 detector — type aliases and generic types are silently skipped

suggested_actions.description: Rename the variable to match the type's shape, or change the type to match the name. If the project intentionally diverges, add the name to `detectors.options.singular_plural_type_mismatch.allowedNames`.

findings.summary: 4 TODO/FIXME markers (12.9 per 1k LOC). Each marker is intended behaviour the author flagged as not-final — too many in one file makes it hard for an agent to tell which logic is load-bearing.

suggested_actions.description: Convert each marker into a tracked issue or remove it. Lingering TODOs mislead humans and agents about which behaviour is intentional.

findings.summary: Route /account/subscription appears to be unreachable from any nav, doc link, or in-repo import. The page may be in flight, or it may have outlived the journey it was built for.
evidence: no nav source references this destination
no internal doc link references this destination
no in-repo import targets this route file

suggested_actions.description: Either link the destination from the relevant nav / docs, or remove it if the flow it served is gone.

findings.summary: Route /team appears to be unreachable from any nav, doc link, or in-repo import. The page may be in flight, or it may have outlived the journey it was built for.
evidence: no nav source references this destination
no internal doc link references this destination
no in-repo import targets this route file

suggested_actions.description: Either link the destination from the relevant nav / docs, or remove it if the flow it served is gone.

findings.summary: Route /workspace/members appears to be unreachable from any nav, doc link, or in-repo import. The page may be in flight, or it may have outlived the journey it was built for.
evidence: no nav source references this destination
no internal doc link references this destination
no in-repo import targets this route file

suggested_actions.description: Either link the destination from the relevant nav / docs, or remove it if the flow it served is gone.
